body.title: agent_datacenter: skeleton tests — registration, health rollup, collision
body.result: tests/test_skeleton.py: 14 tests — registration, deregistration, reattach, collision (online+offline), health rollup (healthy/error/timeout), auth (skeleton/self allowed, third-party raises). All pass.
body.description:
Tests for the skeleton's core behaviors: device registration, health rollup, and namespace collision hard-fail.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/tests/test_skeleton.py:
  def test_register_device(): register StubDevice → appears in rack.devices()
  def test_deregister_device(): register then deregister → status='offline' in rack.devices()
  def test_namespace_collision(): register StubDevice twice with same id → RegistrationError
  def test_health_rollup_healthy(): register 1 healthy StubDevice → rack.health()['stub'] == {healthy: True}
  def test_health_rollup_offline(): device goes down → rack.health()['stub'] == {healthy: False, error: 'timeout'}
  def test_skeleton_self_registers(): skeleton starts → rack.devices() contains 'skeleton'
  All tests use StubDevice from fixtures; no real Postgres or IMAP needed

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/tests/test_skeleton.py

Design rules:
- No real external deps in skeleton tests — StubDevice and mock IMAP server only

Scope boundary:
IN: registration, deregistration, collision, health rollup tests using stubs
OUT: integration tests with real Postgres or IMAP (those are separate integration test files)

Test plan:
- pytest tests/test_skeleton.py passes cleanly without Postgres or Dovecot running
- All 6 test functions present and passing
